channel.title: Use a robots.txt file in the root
channel.description:
**--- [Feature Card](https://www.notion.so/frontity/Use-a-robots-txt-file-in-the-root-13e32cf0fe6e4afba4b225c923f36b69)**
<!-- Please, follow this template in order to be as efficient as possible. Before opening a new feature, please check that it hasn't been already added. -->
## Description Right now we are returning a default `robots.txt` file: https://github.com/frontity/frontity/blob/dev/packages/core/src/server/index.tsx#L31-L36
Even though *we want to provide a Frontity package to configure the `robots.txt` file using the Frontity settings in the future*, it'd be great to be able to configure it using a file in your root directory as well, similar to [what we are doing for the `favicon.ico`](https://github.com/frontity/frontity/blob/dev/packages/core/src/server/index.tsx#L46-L47).
## User Stories
> As a **Frontity project developer** > I want to **use a robots.txt file** > so that **I can get control of my robots easily**
## Possible solution
I guess we have to check if the file exists first. If it does return it and if it doesn't, return the default string like we are doing right now.

item.title: Use a robots.txt file in the root
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
This has been included in the latest release! Thanks @koli14 for implementing it

You can now add the robots.txt file at the root of your theme (next to the frontity.settings.js file) and when you build and deploy your app for production, it will be automatically picked up by the Frontity server and served at https://your-site-url.xyx/robots.txt.

Read full topic
item.source: Use a robots.txt file in the root

item.title: Use a robots.txt file in the root
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
christian.nascimento:

Is not possible to use now.json routes to get this file?

I’m sorry we didn’t answer you @christian.nascimento!

Read full topic
item.source: Use a robots.txt file in the root

item.title: Use a robots.txt file in the root
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
@koli14 has just made a pull request to implement this feature

github.com/frontity/frontity

Add support to use robots.txt in the root

frontity:dev ← Koli14:dev
opened 09:18AM - 06 May 20 UTC
Koli14
+15 -5

Moving this to Currently in Development.

Read full topic
item.source: Use a robots.txt file in the root

item.title: Use a robots.txt file in the root
item.dc:creator: christian.nascimento
item.description:
Is not possible to use now.json routes to get this file? Right now I need to put some redirects in robots.txt

Read full topic
item.source: Use a robots.txt file in the root

item.title: Use a robots.txt file in the root
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
— Feature Card

Description

Right now we are returning a default robots.txt file:

github.com

frontity/frontity/blob/dev/packages/core/src/server/index.tsx#L31-L36

    

  1. // Serve static files.
    2. 

  2. app.use(async (ctx, next) => {
    3. 

  3.   const moduleStats = await getStats({ target: "module" });
    4. 

  4.   const es5Stats = await getStats({ target: "es5" });
    5. 

  5.   const stats = moduleStats || es5Stats;
    6. 

  6. 

    7.

Even though we want to provide a Frontity package to configure the robots.txt file using the Frontity settings in the future, it’d be great to be able to configure it using a file in your root directory as well, similar to what we are doing for the favicon.ico.

User Stories

As a Frontity project developer
I want to use a robots.txt file
so that I can get control of my robots easily

Possible solution

I guess we have to check if the file exists first. If it does return it and if it doesn’t, return the default string like we are doing right now.

Read full topic
item.source: Use a robots.txt file in the root
